close_reason: Added cached ro_session with take/return helpers for session reuse
description:
Trigger: high-throughput signing — e.g. a JWT issuer signing 1000 tokens/second.

Bad behavior: each call to sign(), get(), put(), public_key_der() opens a new C_OpenSession + C_Login + does work + drops session (close on drop). For SoftHSM2 a session open+login is ~100µs; for network-backed HSMs (CloudHSM, gcp-kms-pkcs11) it can be 5-50ms per session because each session may involve a TLS handshake or auth round-trip.

At scale this dominates throughput. Comparing to secretx-wolfhsm which caches an open client and reuses across calls, secretx-pkcs11 has no session reuse.

Fix: cache a session per Pkcs11Backend instance, protected by a Mutex (or a small pool of sessions for parallelism). PKCS#11 sessions are stateful — sign init/update/final pairs must stay on one session — but a bare 'one-shot sign' uses a single C_Sign call so a per-call lock is fine. Session reuse breaks if the HSM goes away and reconnects, so add session validity checking via a CKR_SESSION_HANDLE_INVALID -> reopen retry path.
title: Every sign/get/put/public_key_der opens a fresh PKCS#11 session
